prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `68bc2e87`.
codex_response:
The task rule is: treat each non-background color as a partially occluded rectangular perimeter, recover which later rectangles overwrote which earlier borders, and output the colors as a single vertical column in that dependency order. The summary hint was usable, but I tightened it: the reliable signal is occluded-border ordering, not just naive bbox nesting.

The new package is [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_68bc2e87/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_68bc2e87/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_68bc2e87/generator.py#L179). No new shared primitive was needed, and no `notes.md` was necessary because the hint was consistent with the official examples.

The generator builds larger one-cell rectangular chains on an `18x19` `8` background, with controlled border-overwrite dependencies and an internal verifier gate so only unambiguous layouts survive. Validation passed: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/previews/preview_01.png).

Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/68bc2e87/previews/preview_03.png).
